questions.q: Which leader is chiefly credited with integrating the princely states into independent India?
questions.explanation: Sardar Patel, as Home Minister, led the integration of the princely states and is called the 'Iron Man of India.'

questions.q: Which senior civil servant worked closely with Patel as the key administrator of states integration?
questions.explanation: V. P. Menon, Secretary of the States Department, was Patel's principal aide in negotiating the accession of the states.

questions.q: Roughly how many princely states existed at the time of independence in 1947?
questions.explanation: There were over 500 princely states of varying size that had to accede to India or Pakistan in 1947.

questions.q: What legal document did rulers sign to join the Indian Union?
questions.explanation: Princely rulers signed the Instrument of Accession to join either India or Pakistan.

questions.q: Which department of the new government handled the integration of the princely states?
questions.explanation: The States Department, headed by Patel with Menon as secretary, managed relations with and the accession of the states.

questions.q: At independence the princely states were, in legal terms, given the choice to accede to India, accede to Pakistan, or:
questions.explanation: With the lapse of British paramountcy, states were theoretically free to accede to either dominion or remain independent, though Patel pressed them to join India.

questions.q: Which large southern princely state's ruler initially refused to join India and was integrated by force in 1948?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad, ruled by the Nizam, was integrated into India through a military action in September 1948.

questions.q: The September 1948 military action that integrated Hyderabad was code-named:
questions.explanation: The Indian army's police action against Hyderabad in 1948 was code-named Operation Polo.

questions.q: What title did Sardar Patel earn for his role in unifying the states?
questions.explanation: Patel is honoured as the Iron Man of India for forging the fragmented states into a single Union.

questions.q: The princely state of Junagadh's ruler first acceded to Pakistan, but the state ultimately joined which country?
questions.explanation: Junagadh's accession to Pakistan was reversed and the state joined India following a plebiscite in 1948.

questions.q: Patel took charge of the States Department in which year, just before independence?
questions.explanation: Patel headed the newly created States Department from mid-1947 to manage the accession of the states.

questions.q: Most princely states were persuaded to accede to India by the deadline of:
questions.explanation: Patel and Menon secured the accession of the great majority of states before independence on 15 August 1947.

questions.q: To incentivize rulers to accede, the government initially offered them a financial allowance known as the:
questions.explanation: Acceding rulers were guaranteed privy purses and certain privileges, which were later abolished in 1971.

questions.q: The three subjects initially surrendered to the central government under the Instrument of Accession were defence, external affairs, and:
questions.explanation: Rulers acceded on defence, external affairs, and communications, retaining internal autonomy at first before fuller integration.

questions.q: Which Hindu-majority state with a Muslim ruler became a flashpoint, leading to its ruler acceding to India in late 1947?
questions.explanation: Jammu and Kashmir's Maharaja Hari Singh acceded to India in October 1947 after a tribal invasion from Pakistan.

questions.q: The Nizam of Hyderabad who resisted accession to India bore which dynastic title?
questions.explanation: The Nizams of Hyderabad belonged to the Asaf Jahi dynasty; the last ruling Nizam was Mir Osman Ali Khan.

questions.q: Many smaller states were grouped together into new administrative units. One such union of Rajput states was called:
questions.explanation: Numerous Rajput princely states were merged to form the union of Rajasthan as part of the integration process.

questions.q: The union of Patiala and East Punjab States, a grouping formed during integration, was abbreviated as:
questions.explanation: PEPSU, the Patiala and East Punjab States Union, brought together several Punjab princely states.

questions.q: Which dewan of Travancore declared the state's intention to remain independent before it acceded to India?
questions.explanation: Travancore's dewan Sir C. P. Ramaswami Iyer initially asserted independence before the state acceded to India in 1947.

questions.q: The integration of the princely states is often compared to the unification of which European nation under a strong statesman?
questions.explanation: Patel's unification of India's states is frequently likened to Bismarck's unification of Germany.

questions.q: Which large central Indian state ruled by the Scindia dynasty acceded peacefully to India?
questions.explanation: Gwalior, ruled by the Scindias, acceded to India and was later merged into Madhya Bharat.

questions.q: The portfolio under which Patel directed the integration also made him independent India's first:
options: Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister
questions.explanation: Patel served as independent India's first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister while integrating the states.

questions.q: The Junagadh plebiscite that confirmed its accession to India was held in which year?
questions.explanation: A plebiscite in February 1948 overwhelmingly favoured Junagadh joining India after its Nawab had fled.

questions.q: The Standstill Agreement that Hyderabad signed with India in 1947 was meant to last for how long while talks continued?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad and India signed a one-year Standstill Agreement in November 1947, but negotiations broke down before Operation Polo in 1948.

questions.q: Which communist-led peasant uprising in the Telangana region complicated Hyderabad's situation before integration?
questions.explanation: The Telangana Rebellion against feudal landlords and the Nizam added to the turmoil in Hyderabad before its 1948 integration.

questions.q: The paramilitary force loyal to the Nizam that resisted integration in Hyderabad was known as the:
questions.explanation: The Razakars, a militia led by Kasim Razvi, resisted Hyderabad's accession and committed atrocities, prompting Operation Polo.

questions.q: The accession of Jammu and Kashmir was accepted by which Governor-General on behalf of India in 1947?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten, as Governor-General of India, accepted the Instrument of Accession of Jammu and Kashmir in October 1947.

questions.q: The privy purses guaranteed to former rulers were finally abolished by a constitutional amendment in which year?
questions.explanation: The privy purses and princely privileges were abolished by the 26th Constitutional Amendment in 1971.

questions.q: Which northeastern princely state's merger with India was completed in 1949 through an agreement signed by its Maharani Regent?
questions.explanation: Tripura's Maharani Regent, Kanchan Prabha Devi, signed the merger agreement in September 1949, and the state's merger with India took effect in October 1949.

questions.q: V. P. Menon co-authored an authoritative account of the process titled:
options: The Story of the Integration of the Indian States
questions.explanation: V. P. Menon wrote 'The Story of the Integration of the Indian States,' a key firsthand record of the merger of the states.
